title: ECC and DDR5
excerpt: Hamming Codes ECC RAM corrects errors that occur in memory before it gets to the CPU. The most common form of ECC is the Hamming Code [1] which when it has R redundant bits can correct single bit e…
content_html:
Hamming Codes

ECC RAM corrects errors that occur in memory before it gets to the CPU. The most common form of ECC is the Hamming Code [1] which when it has R redundant bits can correct single bit errors and detect double-bit errors in messages with 2^R-R-1 bits of data. For PC use that means if you want to protect 32bits of data you need R=6 and with 64bits you need R=7. The standard for DDR4 and similar RAM is 72 bits of data width on the bus and Hamming codes to correct single bit errors and detect double bit errors for 65bits of data. The computers we use have 64bits of data so that allows an extra bit that could be an extra parity, I don’t know what if anything is done with this extra bit.

RDIMM vs UDIMM

One point of confusion in such things is the difference between Registered memory AKA RDIMMs [2] and regular PC/laptop memory which is often referred to as UDIMMs. The “register” is just a buffer which due to complex issues that aren’t relevant to this post means that DIMMs can be larger and you can have more DIMMs in a system but latency may be slightly worse. It is technically quite possible to create RDIMMs without ECC (64bits wide instead of 72) but I have never seen a system that used such RAM.

I have used more than a few systems with ECC UDIMMs and I recommend avoiding them if convenient as ECC UDIMMs are expensive on the second hand market while ECC RDIMMs can get very cheap. There are servers with ECC RDIMMs that are very unsuitable for home use (such as dual-CPU 1RU servers which are very noisy) so once they are past the 5 year tax write-off period the server chassis gets sent to ewaste and the RAM goes on the second hand market, the glut of RAM without systems to use it forces the price down.

For the systems most commonly seen there are RDIMM systems with ECC and UDIMM systems without ECC.

Chipkill

If every bit in RAM was independent of every other bit then the basic Hamming code would solve most problems. However multiple bits in the same chip may be affected by the same problem, or one chip on the DIMM might entirely fail. With every RDIMM having 18 or 36 DRAM chips there are 2 or 4 bits per chip. On DIMMs with 36 DRAM chips one chip could fail and have the errors reliably detected with a Hamming code. On DIMMs with 18 DRAM chips one failed chip can’t necessarily be detected with Hamming codes. IBM trademarked the term ChipKill for ECC systems which can cope with a single DRAM chip failing [3]. This is referred to as “Advanced ECC” on Dell and HP servers which require an even number of DIMMs. If anyone knows what coding method is used for “ChipKill” type systems then please let me know.

Systems with advanced ECC also often have features like hot-spare for RAM and RAID-1 type functionality which is interesting but not something most people who read my blog will ever want to use.

DDR5

DDR5 has on-die ECC to deal with the increased error incidence from smaller and faster memory [4], this is specified as 8 bits of error correction per 128 bits of data which implies basic Hamming codes.

The on-die ECC is not a replacement for regular ECC, it’s a mitigation for new problems introduced. My experience of memory errors is that the majority of repeatable errors (where a system would get an error with Memtest86+ or an ECC error report repeatedly) were DIMM seating issues, I could unplug and reinsert the DIMM in question and then the same tests would pass. Those errors would not be affected by on-die ECC.

One thing that concerns me is the possibility of on-die ECC interacting with ECC on the motherboard and reducing it’s effectiveness. I haven’t been able to find out enough about how this works to determine if that’s the case. My concern is that an error of 3+ bits that’s corrected with a basic Hamming code might be more likely to create an error condition that “Advanced ECC” can’t fix than the original error.

Currently the best published research on the effectiveness of ECC on RAM errors is the Google paper published in 2009 which is based on DDR and DDR2 RAM [5]. So I don’t expect that we will see published research about even DDR4 ECC any time soon. I presume that Google and the other cloud providers are still doing such research and providing the information to DRAM vendors under NDA so we have to just hope that the DRAM vendors do what’s required to make things work correctly and allow us to buy products based on that research.

DDR5 EC4 vs EC8

DDR5 supports 2*32bit “subchannels” instead of just supporting 64bit words [6]. For DDR5 ECC RAM there are variants EC4 which has 36bits of data per subchannel and EC8 which has 40 bits. EC8 allows Hamming codes on each subchannel indepdendently. I haven’t found a reference on how exactly EC4 works, it could be reading 64bits at a time (not taking advantage of the subchannels) to use Hamming codes or it could have 1 parity bit for each subchannel and just assume that there’s no need to check Hamming codes unless the subchannel parity fails. EC8 allows full Hamming code checks on 32bits of data and presumably ChipKill on 64bits.

It’s widely claimed that all DDR5 RDIMMs are EC8 and all DDR5 ECC UDIMMs are EC4. A quick search on ebay turned up adverts for EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs and links to apparently reliable sites confirming that some of the RDIMMs are EC4. There are reports of EC8 UDIMMs even though I couldn’t find any advertised. This seems to mirror the situation with DDR4 where non-ECC RDIMMs are apparently available somewhere and ECC UDIMMs are something I’ve used a few times but most people have never seen.

I then searched for information on what servers support. The Dell R760 server supports both EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs but you can’t have both in the same system.

The existence of EC4 DIMMs is wrong. They shouldn’t make substandard gear, the manufacturing price difference between 72 and 80 bit wide DIMMs isn’t going to be great and the end result is some systems with inadequate specs and extra difficulty in upgrading systems with more things to check for compatibility.

Why ECC is Needed

Here’s an interesting article about Mozilla’s claim that 15% of Firefox crashes are due to RAM hardware errors [7], this seems to be based on repeatable errors and therefore won’t count errors where a bit flip happens once a day or less.

Some years ago I reported a BTRFS corruption issue on my desktop PC to the BTRFS developers and one of them stated that the corruption in question didn’t match any pattern expected from a BTRFS bug and recommended that I run Memtest86+. The memory test revealed that I was getting about one memory corruption per 5 hours so if I had used Firefox on that system any crashes probably wouldn’t have been regarded as hardware errors with RAM. Those errors caused filesystem corruption and some data loss, if I hadn’t been using BTRFS that could have gone unnoticed for years.

On another occasion I had a VM I was using for testing software I was developing that had some unexpected errors. After working on it for a day I had shared the errors with a mailing list of other developers who also spent some time investigating it. Eventually I began to suspect a hardware problem, I went on site and when I rebooted the system to run Memtest86+ it didn’t even boot as it had errors that stopped the BIOS from even working correctly. It was strange that the system was apparently working correctly and restarting the KVM VM resulted in the same errors happening in the same code and nothing else on the VM apparently having a problem. It turned out that the system had a motherboard problem that made all but one of the DIMM sockets unusable so I ended up sending it to e-waste. That wasted a day of my time and some hours of other people’s time. Presumably on other occasions developer time is wasted due to hardware errors and no-one even realises.

What Society Needs

We need ECC RAM to be more widely used. Ideally we would have some government action to force this given the ongoing cost to society in corrupted data and lost time due to RAM hardware errors. I think that at minimum we need sufficient taxes on non-ECC RAM (and EC4 RAM for DDR5) to make it more expensive when bought new than ECC RAM.

We need to have greater knowledge of the benefits of ECC RAM among computer experts, people need to recommend that computers be purchased with ECC RAM whenever possible and that systems which can’t have ECC RAM (laptops and phones) shouldn’t be used for storing important data.

We need to avoid silly things like having so many variants of RAM to confuse people and make it needlessly difficult to get ECC RAM working.

  • [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamming_code
  • [2] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Registered_memory
  • [3] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chipkill
  • [4] https://tinyurl.com/2aj7oztw
  • [5] https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~bianca/papers/sigmetrics09.pdf
  • [6] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DDR5_SDRAM#Subchannels
  • [7] https://tinyurl.com/yv4of7hz

blocks.html_en: Hamming Codes

sentences.en: Hamming Codes
sentences.zh: 汉明码

blocks.html_en: ECC RAM corrects errors that occur in memory before it gets to the CPU. The most common form of ECC is the Hamming Code [1] which when it has R redundant bits can correct single bit errors and detect double-bit errors in messages with 2^R-R-1 bits of data. For PC use that means if you want to protect 32bits of data you need R=6 and with 64bits you need R=7. The standard for DDR4 and similar RAM is 72 bits of data width on the bus and Hamming codes to correct single bit errors and detect double bit errors for 65bits of data. The computers we use have 64bits of data so that allows an extra bit that could be an extra parity, I don’t know what if anything is done with this extra bit.

sentences.en: ECC RAM corrects errors that occur in memory before it gets to the CPU.
sentences.zh: ECC RAM 可在内存到达 CPU 之前纠正内存中发生的错误。

sentences.en: The most common form of ECC is the Hamming Code [1] which when it has R redundant bits can correct single bit errors and detect double-bit errors in messages with 2^R-R-1 bits of data.
sentences.zh: ECC 最常见的形式是汉明码 [1]，当它具有 R 个冗余位时，可以纠正单位错误并检测具有 2^R-R-1 位数据的消息中的双位错误。

sentences.en: For PC use that means if you want to protect 32bits of data you need R=6 and with 64bits you need R=7.
sentences.zh: 对于 PC 使用，这意味着如果您想保护 32 位数据，您需要 R=6，而对于 64 位数据，您需要 R=7。

sentences.en: The standard for DDR4 and similar RAM is 72 bits of data width on the bus and Hamming codes to correct single bit errors and detect double bit errors for 65bits of data.
sentences.zh: DDR4 和类似 RAM 的标准是总线上的 72 位数据宽度和汉明码，以纠正单位错误并检测 65 位数据的双位错误。

sentences.en: The computers we use have 64bits of data so that allows an extra bit that could be an extra parity, I don’t know what if anything is done with this extra bit.
sentences.zh: 我们使用的计算机有 64 位数据，因此允许一个额外的位，这可能是一个额外的奇偶校验，我不知道如果用这个额外的位做任何事情会怎样。

sentences.en: RDIMM vs UDIMM
sentences.zh: RDIMM 与 UDIMM

sentences.zh: 此类事情中令人困惑的一点是注册内存（又称为 RDIMM）[2] 与常规 PC/笔记本电脑内存（通常称为 UDIMM）之间的区别。

sentences.en: The “register” is just a buffer which due to complex issues that aren’t relevant to this post means that DIMMs can be larger and you can have more DIMMs in a system but latency may be slightly worse.
sentences.zh: “寄存器”只是一个缓冲区，由于与本文无关的复杂问题，意味着 DIMM 可以更大，并且您可以在系统中拥有更多 DIMM，但延迟可能会稍差一些。

sentences.en: It is technically quite possible to create RDIMMs without ECC (64bits wide instead of 72) but I have never seen a system that used such RAM.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，创建没有 ECC 的 RDIMM（64 位宽而不是 72 位宽）是完全可能的，但我从未见过使用此类 RAM 的系统。

blocks.html_en: I have used more than a few systems with ECC UDIMMs and I recommend avoiding them if convenient as ECC UDIMMs are expensive on the second hand market while ECC RDIMMs can get very cheap. sentences.en: I have used more than a few systems with ECC UDIMMs and I recommend avoiding them if convenient as ECC UDIMMs are expensive on the second hand market while ECC RDIMMs can get very cheap.
sentences.zh: 我使用过多个带有 ECC UDIMM 的系统，如果方便的话，我建议避免使用它们，因为 ECC UDIMM 在二手市场上价格昂贵，而 ECC RDIMM 则非常便宜。

sentences.en: There are servers with ECC RDIMMs that are very unsuitable for home use (such as dual-CPU 1RU servers which are very noisy) so once they are past the 5 year tax write-off period the server chassis gets sent to ewaste and the RAM goes on the second hand market, the glut of RAM without systems to use it forces the price down.
sentences.zh: 有些带有 ECC RDIMM 的服务器非常不适合家庭使用（例如双 CPU 1RU 服务器，噪音非常大），因此一旦过了 5 年税务核销期，服务器机箱就会被送去报废，而 RAM 则进入二手市场，没有系统使用的 RAM 过剩会迫使价格下降。

blocks.html_en: For the systems most commonly seen there are RDIMM systems with ECC and UDIMM systems without ECC.

sentences.en: For the systems most commonly seen there are RDIMM systems with ECC and UDIMM systems without ECC.
sentences.zh: 对于最常见的系统，有带 ECC 的 RDIMM 系统和不带 ECC 的 UDIMM 系统。

blocks.html_en: Chipkill

sentences.en: Chipkill
sentences.zh: 奇普基尔

blocks.html_en: If every bit in RAM was independent of every other bit then the basic Hamming code would solve most problems. However multiple bits in the same chip may be affected by the same problem, or one chip on the DIMM might entirely fail. With every RDIMM having 18 or 36 DRAM chips there are 2 or 4 bits per chip. On DIMMs with 36 DRAM chips one chip could fail and have the errors reliably detected with a Hamming code. sentences.en: If every bit in RAM was independent of every other bit then the basic Hamming code would solve most problems.
sentences.zh: 如果 RAM 中的每个位都独立于其他位，那么基本的汉明码就可以解决大多数问题。

sentences.en: However multiple bits in the same chip may be affected by the same problem, or one chip on the DIMM might entirely fail.
sentences.zh: 然而，同一芯片中的多个位可能会受到同一问题的影响，或者 DIMM 上的一个芯片可能会完全故障。

sentences.en: With every RDIMM having 18 or 36 DRAM chips there are 2 or 4 bits per chip.
sentences.zh: 每个 RDIMM 都有 18 或 36 个 DRAM 芯片，每个芯片有 2 或 4 位。

sentences.en: On DIMMs with 36 DRAM chips one chip could fail and have the errors reliably detected with a Hamming code.
sentences.zh: 在具有 36 个 DRAM 芯片的 DIMM 上，一个芯片可能会发生故障，并且可以通过汉明码可靠地检测到错误。

sentences.en: On DIMMs with 18 DRAM chips one failed chip can’t necessarily be detected with Hamming codes.
sentences.zh: 在具有 18 个 DRAM 芯片的 DIMM 上，不一定可以使用汉明码检测到发生故障的芯片。

sentences.en: IBM trademarked the term ChipKill for ECC systems which can cope with a single DRAM chip failing [3].
sentences.zh: IBM 为 ECC 系统注册了术语 ChipKill 的商标，该系统可以应对单个 DRAM 芯片故障 [3]。

sentences.en: This is referred to as “Advanced ECC” on Dell and HP servers which require an even number of DIMMs.
sentences.zh: 这在需要偶数个 DIMM 的 Dell 和 HP 服务器上称为“高级 ECC”。

sentences.en: If anyone knows what coding method is used for “ChipKill” type systems then please let me know.
sentences.zh: 如果有人知道“ChipKill”类型系统使用什么编码方法，请告诉我。

blocks.html_en: Systems with advanced ECC also often have features like hot-spare for RAM and RAID-1 type functionality which is interesting but not something most people who read my blog will ever want to use.

sentences.en: Systems with advanced ECC also often have features like hot-spare for RAM and RAID-1 type functionality which is interesting but not something most people who read my blog will ever want to use.
sentences.zh: 具有高级 ECC 的系统通常还具有 RAM 热备用和 RAID-1 类型功能等功能，这很有趣，但不是大多数阅读我的博客的人想要使用的功能。

blocks.html_en: DDR5

DDR5 has on-die ECC to deal with the increased error incidence from smaller and faster memory [4], this is specified as 8 bits of error correction per 128 bits of data which implies basic Hamming codes.

sentences.en: DDR5 has on-die ECC to deal with the increased error incidence from smaller and faster memory [4], this is specified as 8 bits of error correction per 128 bits of data which implies basic Hamming codes.
sentences.zh: DDR5 具有片上 ECC 来处理更小、更快的内存 [4] 带来的增加的错误发生率，这被指定为每 128 位数据 8 位错误纠正，这意味着基本的汉明码。

My experience of memory errors is that the majority of repeatable errors (where a system would get an error with Memtest86+ or an ECC error report repeatedly) were DIMM seating issues, I could unplug and reinsert the DIMM in question and then the same tests would pass. Those errors would not be affected by on-die ECC.

sentences.en: The on-die ECC is not a replacement for regular ECC, it’s a mitigation for new problems introduced.
sentences.zh: 片上 ECC 并不是常规 ECC 的替代品，而是对引入的新问题的缓解措施。

sentences.en: My experience of memory errors is that the majority of repeatable errors (where a system would get an error with Memtest86+ or an ECC error report repeatedly) were DIMM seating issues, I could unplug and reinsert the DIMM in question and then the same tests would pass.
sentences.zh: 我对内存错误的经验是，大多数可重复错误（系统会反复出现 Memtest86+ 错误或 ECC 错误报告）都是 DIMM 固定问题，我可以拔下并重新插入有问题的 DIMM，然后相同的测试就会通过。

sentences.en: Those errors would not be affected by on-die ECC.
sentences.zh: 这些错误不会受到片上 ECC 的影响。

blocks.html_en: One thing that concerns me is the possibility of on-die ECC interacting with ECC on the motherboard and reducing it’s effectiveness. I haven’t been able to find out enough about how this works to determine if that’s the case. My concern is that an error of 3+ bits that’s corrected with a basic Hamming code might be more likely to create an error condition that “Advanced ECC” can’t fix than the original error.

sentences.en: One thing that concerns me is the possibility of on-die ECC interacting with ECC on the motherboard and reducing it’s effectiveness.
sentences.zh: 我担心的一件事是片上 ECC 与主板上的 ECC 交互并降低其有效性的可能性。

sentences.en: I haven’t been able to find out enough about how this works to determine if that’s the case.
sentences.zh: 我无法充分了解其工作原理，无法确定情况是否如此。

sentences.en: My concern is that an error of 3+ bits that’s corrected with a basic Hamming code might be more likely to create an error condition that “Advanced ECC” can’t fix than the original error.
sentences.zh: 我担心的是，使用基本汉明码纠正的 3 位以上错误可能比原始错误更有可能产生“高级 ECC”无法修复的错误条件。

blocks.html_en: Currently the best published research on the effectiveness of ECC on RAM errors is the Google paper published in 2009 which is based on DDR and DDR2 RAM [5]. So I don’t expect that we will see published research about even DDR4 ECC any time soon. I presume that Google and the other cloud providers are still doing such research and providing the information to DRAM vendors under NDA so we have to just hope that the DRAM vendors do what’s required to make things work correctly and allow us to buy products based on that research.

sentences.en: Currently the best published research on the effectiveness of ECC on RAM errors is the Google paper published in 2009 which is based on DDR and DDR2 RAM [5].
sentences.zh: 目前关于 ECC 对 RAM 错误有效性的最佳发表研究是 2009 年发表的 Google 论文，该论文基于 DDR 和 DDR2 RAM [5]。

sentences.en: So I don’t expect that we will see published research about even DDR4 ECC any time soon.
sentences.zh: 因此，我预计我们不会很快看到有关 DDR4 ECC 的研究成果。

sentences.en: I presume that Google and the other cloud providers are still doing such research and providing the information to DRAM vendors under NDA so we have to just hope that the DRAM vendors do what’s required to make things work correctly and allow us to buy products based on that research.
sentences.zh: 我认为 Google 和其他云提供商仍在进行此类研究，并根据 NDA 向 DRAM 供应商提供信息，因此我们只能希望 DRAM 供应商采取必要措施以使事情正常工作，并允许我们购买基于该研究的产品。

blocks.html_en: DDR5 EC4 vs EC8

sentences.en: DDR5 EC4 vs EC8
sentences.zh: DDR5 EC4 与 EC8

blocks.html_en: DDR5 supports 2*32bit “subchannels” instead of just supporting 64bit words [6]. For DDR5 ECC RAM there are variants EC4 which has 36bits of data per subchannel and EC8 which has 40 bits. EC8 allows Hamming codes on each subchannel indepdendently. I haven’t found a reference on how exactly EC4 works, it could be reading 64bits at a time (not taking advantage of the subchannels) to use Hamming codes or it could have 1 parity bit for each subchannel and just assume that there’s no need to check Hamming codes unless the subchannel parity fails. EC8 allows full Hamming code checks on 32bits of data and presumably ChipKill on 64bits.

sentences.en: DDR5 supports 2*32bit “subchannels” instead of just supporting 64bit words [6].
sentences.zh: DDR5 支持 2*32 位“子通道”，而不仅仅是支持 64 位字 [6]。

sentences.en: For DDR5 ECC RAM there are variants EC4 which has 36bits of data per subchannel and EC8 which has 40 bits.
sentences.zh: 对于 DDR5 ECC RAM，有 EC4 变体，每个子通道有 36 位数据，EC8 有 40 位数据。

sentences.en: EC8 allows Hamming codes on each subchannel indepdendently.
sentences.zh: EC8 允许在每个子通道上独立进行汉明码。

sentences.en: I haven’t found a reference on how exactly EC4 works, it could be reading 64bits at a time (not taking advantage of the subchannels) to use Hamming codes or it could have 1 parity bit for each subchannel and just assume that there’s no need to check Hamming codes unless the subchannel parity fails.
sentences.zh: 我还没有找到关于 EC4 究竟如何工作的参考资料，它可能一次读取 64 位（不利用子通道）来使用汉明码，或者它可能为每个子通道有 1 个奇偶校验位，并且假设不需要检查汉明码，除非子通道奇偶校验失败。

sentences.en: EC8 allows full Hamming code checks on 32bits of data and presumably ChipKill on 64bits.
sentences.zh: EC8 允许对 32 位数据进行完整的汉明码检查，并且可能对 64 位数据进行 ChipKill 检查。

blocks.html_en: It’s widely claimed that all DDR5 RDIMMs are EC8 and all DDR5 ECC UDIMMs are EC4. A quick search on ebay turned up adverts for EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs and links to apparently reliable sites confirming that some of the RDIMMs are EC4. There are reports of EC8 UDIMMs even though I couldn’t find any advertised. This seems to mirror the situation with DDR4 where non-ECC RDIMMs are apparently available somewhere and ECC UDIMMs are something I’ve used a few times but most people have never seen.

sentences.en: It’s widely claimed that all DDR5 RDIMMs are EC8 and all DDR5 ECC UDIMMs are EC4.
sentences.zh: 人们普遍认为所有 DDR5 RDIMM 都是 EC8，所有 DDR5 ECC UDIMM 都是 EC4。

sentences.en: A quick search on ebay turned up adverts for EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs and links to apparently reliable sites confirming that some of the RDIMMs are EC4.
sentences.zh: 在 eBay 上快速搜索后，发现了 EC4 和 EC8 RDIMM 的广告，并链接到明显可靠的网站，确认某些 RDIMM 是 EC4。

sentences.en: There are reports of EC8 UDIMMs even though I couldn’t find any advertised.
sentences.zh: 尽管我找不到任何广告，但还是有关于 EC8 UDIMM 的报道。

sentences.en: This seems to mirror the situation with DDR4 where non-ECC RDIMMs are apparently available somewhere and ECC UDIMMs are something I’ve used a few times but most people have never seen.
sentences.zh: 这似乎反映了 DDR4 的情况，非 ECC RDIMM 显然在某处可用，而 ECC UDIMM 是我用过几次但大多数人从未见过的东西。

blocks.html_en: I then searched for information on what servers support. The Dell R760 server supports both EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs but you can’t have both in the same system.

sentences.en: I then searched for information on what servers support.
sentences.zh: 然后我搜索了有关服务器支持的信息。

sentences.en: The Dell R760 server supports both EC4 and EC8 RDIMMs but you can’t have both in the same system.
sentences.zh: Dell R760 服务器同时支持 EC4 和 EC8 RDIMM，但不能在同一系统中同时使用这两种内存。

blocks.html_en: The existence of EC4 DIMMs is wrong. They shouldn’t make substandard gear, the manufacturing price difference between 72 and 80 bit wide DIMMs isn’t going to be great and the end result is some systems with inadequate specs and extra difficulty in upgrading systems with more things to check for compatibility.

sentences.en: The existence of EC4 DIMMs is wrong.
sentences.zh: EC4 DIMM 的存在是错误的。

sentences.en: They shouldn’t make substandard gear, the manufacturing price difference between 72 and 80 bit wide DIMMs isn’t going to be great and the end result is some systems with inadequate specs and extra difficulty in upgrading systems with more things to check for compatibility.
sentences.zh: 他们不应该制造不合格的设备，72 和 80 位宽 DIMM 之间的制造价格差异不会太大，最终结果是一些系统规格不足，并且升级系统时会遇到更多困难，需要检查兼容性。

blocks.html_en: Why ECC is Needed

sentences.en: Why ECC is Needed
sentences.zh: 为什么需要 ECC

blocks.html_en: Here’s an interesting article about Mozilla’s claim that 15% of Firefox crashes are due to RAM hardware errors [7], this seems to be based on repeatable errors and therefore won’t count errors where a bit flip happens once a day or less.

sentences.en: Here’s an interesting article about Mozilla’s claim that 15% of Firefox crashes are due to RAM hardware errors [7], this seems to be based on repeatable errors and therefore won’t count errors where a bit flip happens once a day or less.
sentences.zh: 这里有一篇有趣的文章，内容是 Mozilla 声称 15% 的 Firefox 崩溃是由于 RAM 硬件错误造成的 [7]，这似乎是基于可重复的错误，因此不会计算每天发生一次或更少的位翻转的错误。

blocks.html_en: Some years ago I reported a BTRFS corruption issue on my desktop PC to the BTRFS developers and one of them stated that the corruption in question didn’t match any pattern expected from a BTRFS bug and recommended that I run Memtest86+. The memory test revealed that I was getting about one memory corruption per 5 hours so if I had used Firefox on that system any crashes probably wouldn’t have been regarded as hardware errors with RAM. Those errors caused filesystem corruption and some data loss, if I hadn’t been using BTRFS that could have gone unnoticed for years.

sentences.en: Some years ago I reported a BTRFS corruption issue on my desktop PC to the BTRFS developers and one of them stated that the corruption in question didn’t match any pattern expected from a BTRFS bug and recommended that I run Memtest86+.
sentences.zh: 几年前，我向 BTRFS 开发人员报告了我的台式电脑上的 BTRFS 损坏问题，其中一位开发人员表示，所涉及的损坏与 BTRFS 错误预期的任何模式都不匹配，并建议我运行 Memtest86+。

sentences.en: The memory test revealed that I was getting about one memory corruption per 5 hours so if I had used Firefox on that system any crashes probably wouldn’t have been regarded as hardware errors with RAM.
sentences.zh: 内存测试显示，我每 5 小时就会遇到一次内存损坏，因此，如果我在该系统上使用 Firefox，则任何崩溃可能都不会被视为 RAM 的硬件错误。

sentences.en: Those errors caused filesystem corruption and some data loss, if I hadn’t been using BTRFS that could have gone unnoticed for years.
sentences.zh: 这些错误会导致文件系统损坏和一些数据丢失，如果我没有使用 BTRFS，这些错误可能会被忽视很多年。

blocks.html_en: On another occasion I had a VM I was using for testing software I was developing that had some unexpected errors. After working on it for a day I had shared the errors with a mailing list of other developers who also spent some time investigating it. Eventually I began to suspect a hardware problem, I went on site and when I rebooted the system to run Memtest86+ it didn’t even boot as it had errors that stopped the BIOS from even working correctly. It was strange that the system was apparently working correctly and restarting the KVM VM resulted in the same errors happening in the same code and nothing else on the VM apparently having a problem. It turned out that the system had a motherboard problem that made all but one of the DIMM sockets unusable so I ended up sending it to e-waste. That wasted a day of my time and some hours of other people’s time. Presumably on other occasions developer time is wasted due to hardware errors and no-one even realises.

sentences.en: On another occasion I had a VM I was using for testing software I was developing that had some unexpected errors.
sentences.zh: 还有一次，我使用一台虚拟机来测试我正在开发的软件，但该虚拟机出现了一些意外错误。

sentences.en: After working on it for a day I had shared the errors with a mailing list of other developers who also spent some time investigating it.
sentences.zh: 经过一天的工作后，我与其他开发人员的邮件列表分享了这些错误，他们也花了一些时间调查它。

sentences.en: Eventually I began to suspect a hardware problem, I went on site and when I rebooted the system to run Memtest86+ it didn’t even boot as it had errors that stopped the BIOS from even working correctly.
sentences.zh: 最终我开始怀疑硬件问题，我去了现场，当我重新启动系统以运行 Memtest86+ 时，它甚至无法启动，因为它存在错误，导致 BIOS 甚至无法正常工作。

sentences.en: It was strange that the system was apparently working correctly and restarting the KVM VM resulted in the same errors happening in the same code and nothing else on the VM apparently having a problem.
sentences.zh: 奇怪的是，系统显然工作正常，重新启动 KVM 虚拟机会导致相同代码中发生相同的错误，而虚拟机上的其他任何地方显然都没有问题。

sentences.en: It turned out that the system had a motherboard problem that made all but one of the DIMM sockets unusable so I ended up sending it to e-waste.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，系统出现了主板问题，导致除其中一个 DIMM 插槽外的所有插槽均无法使用，因此我最终将其送往电子垃圾处理厂。

sentences.en: That wasted a day of my time and some hours of other people’s time.
sentences.zh: 这浪费了我一天的时间和其他人几个小时的时间。

sentences.en: Presumably on other occasions developer time is wasted due to hardware errors and no-one even realises.
sentences.zh: 大概在其他情况下，开发人员的时间由于硬件错误而被浪费，甚至没有人意识到。

blocks.html_en: What Society Needs

sentences.en: What Society Needs
sentences.zh: 社会需要什么

blocks.html_en: We need ECC RAM to be more widely used. Ideally we would have some government action to force this given the ongoing cost to society in corrupted data and lost time due to RAM hardware errors. I think that at minimum we need sufficient taxes on non-ECC RAM (and EC4 RAM for DDR5) to make it more expensive when bought new than ECC RAM.

sentences.en: We need ECC RAM to be more widely used.
sentences.zh: 我们需要ECC RAM得到更广泛的应用。

sentences.en: Ideally we would have some government action to force this given the ongoing cost to society in corrupted data and lost time due to RAM hardware errors.
sentences.zh: 理想情况下，考虑到 RAM 硬件错误导致数据损坏和时间损失给社会带来持续成本，我们应该采取一些政府行动来强制执行此操作。

sentences.en: I think that at minimum we need sufficient taxes on non-ECC RAM (and EC4 RAM for DDR5) to make it more expensive when bought new than ECC RAM.
sentences.zh: 我认为至少我们需要对非 ECC RAM（以及 DDR5 的 EC4 RAM）征税，以使其在购买新内存时比 ECC RAM 更贵。

blocks.html_en: We need to have greater knowledge of the benefits of ECC RAM among computer experts, people need to recommend that computers be purchased with ECC RAM whenever possible and that systems which can’t have ECC RAM (laptops and phones) shouldn’t be used for storing important data.

sentences.en: We need to have greater knowledge of the benefits of ECC RAM among computer experts, people need to recommend that computers be purchased with ECC RAM whenever possible and that systems which can’t have ECC RAM (laptops and phones) shouldn’t be used for storing important data.
sentences.zh: 我们需要让计算机专家更多地了解 ECC RAM 的好处，人们需要建议尽可能购买带有 ECC RAM 的计算机，并且不应该使用没有 ECC RAM 的系统（笔记本电脑和手机）来存储重要数据。

blocks.html_en: We need to avoid silly things like having so many variants of RAM to confuse people and make it needlessly difficult to get ECC RAM working.

sentences.en: We need to avoid silly things like having so many variants of RAM to confuse people and make it needlessly difficult to get ECC RAM working.
sentences.zh: 我们需要避免愚蠢的事情，比如有这么多 RAM 变体来迷惑人们，并使 ECC RAM 工作变得不必要的困难。

sentences.en: [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamming_code

content_html_zh:
汉明码

ECC RAM 可在内存到达 CPU 之前纠正内存中发生的错误。 ECC 最常见的形式是汉明码 [1]，当它具有 R 个冗余位时，可以纠正单位错误并检测具有 2^R-R-1 位数据的消息中的双位错误。 对于 PC 使用，这意味着如果您想保护 32 位数据，您需要 R=6，而对于 64 位数据，您需要 R=7。 DDR4 和类似 RAM 的标准是总线上的 72 位数据宽度和汉明码，以纠正单位错误并检测 65 位数据的双位错误。 我们使用的计算机有 64 位数据，因此允许一个额外的位，这可能是一个额外的奇偶校验，我不知道如果用这个额外的位做任何事情会怎样。

RDIMM 与 UDIMM

此类事情中令人困惑的一点是注册内存（又称为 RDIMM）[2] 与常规 PC/笔记本电脑内存（通常称为 UDIMM）之间的区别。 “寄存器”只是一个缓冲区，由于与本文无关的复杂问题，意味着 DIMM 可以更大，并且您可以在系统中拥有更多 DIMM，但延迟可能会稍差一些。 从技术上讲，创建没有 ECC 的 RDIMM（64 位宽而不是 72 位宽）是完全可能的，但我从未见过使用此类 RAM 的系统。

我使用过多个带有 ECC UDIMM 的系统，如果方便的话，我建议避免使用它们，因为 ECC UDIMM 在二手市场上价格昂贵，而 ECC RDIMM 则非常便宜。 有些带有 ECC RDIMM 的服务器非常不适合家庭使用（例如双 CPU 1RU 服务器，噪音非常大），因此一旦过了 5 年税务核销期，服务器机箱就会被送去报废，而 RAM 则进入二手市场，没有系统使用的 RAM 过剩会迫使价格下降。

对于最常见的系统，有带 ECC 的 RDIMM 系统和不带 ECC 的 UDIMM 系统。

奇普基尔

如果 RAM 中的每个位都独立于其他位，那么基本的汉明码就可以解决大多数问题。 然而，同一芯片中的多个位可能会受到同一问题的影响，或者 DIMM 上的一个芯片可能会完全故障。 每个 RDIMM 都有 18 或 36 个 DRAM 芯片，每个芯片有 2 或 4 位。 在具有 36 个 DRAM 芯片的 DIMM 上，一个芯片可能会发生故障，并且可以通过汉明码可靠地检测到错误。 在具有 18 个 DRAM 芯片的 DIMM 上，不一定可以使用汉明码检测到发生故障的芯片。 IBM 为 ECC 系统注册了术语 ChipKill 的商标，该系统可以应对单个 DRAM 芯片故障 [3]。 这在需要偶数个 DIMM 的 Dell 和 HP 服务器上称为“高级 ECC”。 如果有人知道“ChipKill”类型系统使用什么编码方法，请告诉我。

具有高级 ECC 的系统通常还具有 RAM 热备用和 RAID-1 类型功能等功能，这很有趣，但不是大多数阅读我的博客的人想要使用的功能。

DDR5

DDR5 具有片上 ECC 来处理更小、更快的内存 [4] 带来的增加的错误发生率，这被指定为每 128 位数据 8 位错误纠正，这意味着基本的汉明码。

片上 ECC 并不是常规 ECC 的替代品，而是对引入的新问题的缓解措施。 我对内存错误的经验是，大多数可重复错误（系统会反复出现 Memtest86+ 错误或 ECC 错误报告）都是 DIMM 固定问题，我可以拔下并重新插入有问题的 DIMM，然后相同的测试就会通过。 这些错误不会受到片上 ECC 的影响。

我担心的一件事是片上 ECC 与主板上的 ECC 交互并降低其有效性的可能性。 我无法充分了解其工作原理，无法确定情况是否如此。 我担心的是，使用基本汉明码纠正的 3 位以上错误可能比原始错误更有可能产生“高级 ECC”无法修复的错误条件。

目前关于 ECC 对 RAM 错误有效性的最佳发表研究是 2009 年发表的 Google 论文，该论文基于 DDR 和 DDR2 RAM [5]。 因此，我预计我们不会很快看到有关 DDR4 ECC 的研究成果。 我认为 Google 和其他云提供商仍在进行此类研究，并根据 NDA 向 DRAM 供应商提供信息，因此我们只能希望 DRAM 供应商采取必要措施以使事情正常工作，并允许我们购买基于该研究的产品。

DDR5 EC4 与 EC8

DDR5 支持 2*32 位“子通道”，而不仅仅是支持 64 位字 [6]。 对于 DDR5 ECC RAM，有 EC4 变体，每个子通道有 36 位数据，EC8 有 40 位数据。 EC8 允许在每个子通道上独立进行汉明码。 我还没有找到关于 EC4 究竟如何工作的参考资料，它可能一次读取 64 位（不利用子通道）来使用汉明码，或者它可能为每个子通道有 1 个奇偶校验位，并且假设不需要检查汉明码，除非子通道奇偶校验失败。 EC8 允许对 32 位数据进行完整的汉明码检查，并且可能对 64 位数据进行 ChipKill 检查。

人们普遍认为所有 DDR5 RDIMM 都是 EC8，所有 DDR5 ECC UDIMM 都是 EC4。 在 eBay 上快速搜索后，发现了 EC4 和 EC8 RDIMM 的广告，并链接到明显可靠的网站，确认某些 RDIMM 是 EC4。 尽管我找不到任何广告，但还是有关于 EC8 UDIMM 的报道。 这似乎反映了 DDR4 的情况，非 ECC RDIMM 显然在某处可用，而 ECC UDIMM 是我用过几次但大多数人从未见过的东西。

然后我搜索了有关服务器支持的信息。 Dell R760 服务器同时支持 EC4 和 EC8 RDIMM，但不能在同一系统中同时使用这两种内存。

EC4 DIMM 的存在是错误的。 他们不应该制造不合格的设备，72 和 80 位宽 DIMM 之间的制造价格差异不会太大，最终结果是一些系统规格不足，并且升级系统时会遇到更多困难，需要检查兼容性。

为什么需要 ECC

这里有一篇有趣的文章，内容是 Mozilla 声称 15% 的 Firefox 崩溃是由于 RAM 硬件错误造成的 [7]，这似乎是基于可重复的错误，因此不会计算每天发生一次或更少的位翻转的错误。

几年前，我向 BTRFS 开发人员报告了我的台式电脑上的 BTRFS 损坏问题，其中一位开发人员表示，所涉及的损坏与 BTRFS 错误预期的任何模式都不匹配，并建议我运行 Memtest86+。 内存测试显示，我每 5 小时就会遇到一次内存损坏，因此，如果我在该系统上使用 Firefox，则任何崩溃可能都不会被视为 RAM 的硬件错误。 这些错误会导致文件系统损坏和一些数据丢失，如果我没有使用 BTRFS，这些错误可能会被忽视很多年。

还有一次，我使用一台虚拟机来测试我正在开发的软件，但该虚拟机出现了一些意外错误。 经过一天的工作后，我与其他开发人员的邮件列表分享了这些错误，他们也花了一些时间调查它。 最终我开始怀疑硬件问题，我去了现场，当我重新启动系统以运行 Memtest86+ 时，它甚至无法启动，因为它存在错误，导致 BIOS 甚至无法正常工作。 奇怪的是，系统显然工作正常，重新启动 KVM 虚拟机会导致相同代码中发生相同的错误，而虚拟机上的其他任何地方显然都没有问题。 事实证明，系统出现了主板问题，导致除其中一个 DIMM 插槽外的所有插槽均无法使用，因此我最终将其送往电子垃圾处理厂。 这浪费了我一天的时间和其他人几个小时的时间。 大概在其他情况下，开发人员的时间由于硬件错误而被浪费，甚至没有人意识到。

社会需要什么

我们需要ECC RAM得到更广泛的应用。 理想情况下，考虑到 RAM 硬件错误导致数据损坏和时间损失给社会带来持续成本，我们应该采取一些政府行动来强制执行此操作。 我认为至少我们需要对非 ECC RAM（以及 DDR5 的 EC4 RAM）征税，以使其在购买新内存时比 ECC RAM 更贵。

我们需要让计算机专家更多地了解 ECC RAM 的好处，人们需要建议尽可能购买带有 ECC RAM 的计算机，并且不应该使用没有 ECC RAM 的系统（笔记本电脑和手机）来存储重要数据。

我们需要避免愚蠢的事情，比如有这么多 RAM 变体来迷惑人们，并使 ECC RAM 工作变得不必要的困难。
